prompt: This method throws IndexOutOfBoundsException when called with an empty list. What is the bug?
choices: ArrayList.get() requires the index to be passed as a long, not int
The method never checks whether the list is empty before calling get(0)
The default parameter is unused — remove it to fix the signature
List.get() always throws IndexOutOfBoundsException for index 0
explanation: get(0) on an empty list throws IndexOutOfBoundsException because index 0 does not exist. Fix: return items.isEmpty() ? def : items.get(0); — check first, then access.
